differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune encephalitis (e.g., anti-LGI1 or anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis)
Psychogenic nonepileptic seizures (PNES) comorbid with epilepsy
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis (including autoimmune encephalitis panel and paraneoplastic antibodies)
Send serum for autoimmune/paraneoplastic encephalitis panel
Perform continuous video-EEG monitoring to rule out ongoing nonconvulsive status epilepticus
Screen for underlying malignancy (e.g., whole-body PET-CT or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis)
Obtain brain MRI with and without contrast specifically looking for signs of limbic encephalitis
Consult infectious disease for consideration of rare or atypical viral encephalitides
Consider empiric treatment with high-dose corticosteroids or intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) if suspicion for autoimmune encephalitis remains high while awaiting diagnostic results

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune encephalitis (specifically LGI1-antibody encephalitis given the limbic presentation and possible faciobrachial dystonic seizures)
Postictal psychosis secondary to refractory temporal lobe epilepsy
Interictal dysphoric disorder or chronic psychosis of epilepsy
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, including autoimmune encephalitis panel, cell count, protein, and glucose
Obtain serum autoimmune encephalitis panel and paraneoplastic antibody testing
Assess trough levels of all current antiseizure medications and check comprehensive metabolic panel for toxic or metabolic contributors
Maintain continuous video-EEG monitoring to confirm or exclude nonconvulsive status epilepticus during episodes of altered mental status
Perform whole-body malignancy screening (e.g., CT chest/abdomen/pelvis or PET-CT)
Consult psychiatry for management of severe agitation and behavioral safety
Consider early initiation of pulse steroids or IVIG if autoimmune suspicion remains high while awaiting antibody results
